text.title: Quality Management — Agentic Workflow and Subagents
text.summary: DoD-validator and metric-collector agent pipeline, defect triage prompt pattern, and AI-specific failure modes in quality gate automation.

section.title: Agentic Quality Pipeline

rule.statement: Run quality gates twice: pre-commit (fast — lint, format) and pre-merge (full — tests, coverage, security scan); do not duplicate slow checks in both phases.
rule.rationale: Duplicating slow checks in pre-commit kills developer velocity; duplicating only in pre-merge creates late-stage surprises. The split is the correct trade-off.

section.p: A quality-auditor subagent reads changed files in a PR, applies the DoD checklist, runs static analysis, and posts a structured review. A metric-collector aggregates coverage, defect rate, escape rate, and MTTR into a weekly dashboard. Human-in-loop is required for severity classification and any waiver decision.

list.item: dod-validator — checks PR against DoD line items from dod.yaml; posts pass/fail per item with evidence link; blocks merge if any must item fails.
quality-metric-collector — pulls coverage, lint, defect counts, MTTR; emits dashboard JSON for trend visualization.
defect-triager — drafts severity + priority with one-sentence rationale citing user impact, frequency, workaround; awaits human confirmation before assigning.
quality-trend-analyst — weekly run; identifies regressions in any metric; opens incident if SPC control limits breached.

example.note: DoD-validator prompt — blocks on must items, requires evidence links:
example.code: You are a dod-validator subagent. Apply DoD {dod.yaml} to PR diff {diff}. For each item return: {item_id, status: pass|fail|n/a, evidence_link}. Block merge if any "must" item fails. Do not invent evidence. For "n/a" items: flag for human approval — never auto-skip.

example.note: Defect-triager prompt — structured severity output awaiting human confirmation:
example.code: You are a defect-triager. Given bug report {report}, draft: - severity: Critical|High|Medium|Low - priority: P1|P2|P3 - rationale: one sentence citing user impact, frequency, workaround availability - similar_issue_ids: [ ... ] Output JSON. Wait for human confirm before assigning.

antipattern.description: Agent writes assertion-free tests to hit the coverage percentage target.
antipattern.reason: Coverage tools measure lines executed, not assertion correctness. Gate on mutation score (mutmut, stryker) for critical modules to detect tautological tests.

antipattern.description: Defect severity consistently classified as "Medium" by the triager.
antipattern.reason: Medium is the safest answer for an LLM avoiding both over- and under-escalation. Calibrate with rubric examples and force evidence citation per severity choice.

antipattern.description: Agent auto-skips "n/a" DoD items without human approval.
antipattern.reason: N/a bypass is the most exploited DoD escape hatch; require explicit human approval for any n/a tag to preserve the gate's integrity.

antipattern.description: Static analyzer run produces 10,000 legacy issues; agent attempts to fix all in one PR.
antipattern.reason: Bulk fixes in a single PR are impossible to review and routinely introduce regressions. Chunk by file or module with incremental PRs.

list.item: For agent-written code: require 90%+ coverage, mutation testing on critical modules, no warning suppressions without explanatory comment.
Quality cost is U-shaped: too little = rework, too much = lost velocity. Re-tune gates quarterly using lead-time and defect data.
Sentry/BugSnag rate-limits can drop events during incident spikes; do not trust raw counts for SLO breach declarations.
